wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the ExamGuard Pro project overview section focused on the AI-powered exam proctoring system. Explain its core purpose of ensuring academic integrity through multi-modal AI analysis, including face detection, eye gaze tracking, OCR-based content monitoring, and NLP-based plagiarism detection. Document the system architecture showing the integration of backend FastAPI services, React dashboard, Chrome extension, and AI/ML components. Include both conceptual overviews for stakeholders and technical details for developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including terms like 'risk scoring', 'session monitoring', and 'anomaly detection'. Provide practical examples demonstrating the real-time monitoring capabilities and dynamic risk assessment features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for ExamGuard Pro focused on quick setup and initial deployment. Document prerequisites including Python 3.10+, Node.js 18+, Supabase account requirements, and Tesseract OCR installation. Provide step-by-step installation instructions for backend (FastAPI), frontend (React), and Chrome extension components. Include environment variable configuration, database setup with Supabase, and first-time deployment using Render. Document common setup issues and troubleshooting steps. Provide practical examples of running the system locally and verifying successful installation. Include verification commands and expected outputs to confirm proper setup.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the ExamGuard Pro system overview focused on the multi-layered architecture combining backend services, frontend dashboard, Chrome extension, and AI/ML components. Explain the system's purpose as an AI-powered exam proctoring solution that monitors online examinations through multi-modal analysis. Document the high-level design showing how the FastAPI backend serves as the central coordinator, the React dashboard provides real-time monitoring interfaces, the Chrome extension captures browser events, and AI services perform computer vision and NLP analysis. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners explaining the overall system flow and technical details for experienced developers covering component interactions. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including terms like 'secure vision engine', 'real-time monitoring manager', and 'analysis pipeline'. Provide practical examples demonstrating the complete data flow from student exam participation through AI analysis to real-time dashboard alerts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for ExamGuard Pro authentication endpoints focused on user login, registration, and token management. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas for /api/auth/login, /api/auth/register, and /api/auth/token endpoints. Include detailed specifications for JWT token generation, refresh token handling, and session management. Document authentication flows including password-based login, admin registration, and user role-based access control. Provide comprehensive examples of authentication requests, token responses, and error handling for invalid credentials, expired tokens, and unauthorized access. Include security considerations for token storage, CSRF protection, and rate limiting. Address common authentication scenarios, client implementation guidelines for mobile and web applications, and integration patterns with frontend dashboards.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive component architecture documentation for the ExamGuard Pro React dashboard. Document the main layout structure including AppLayout, Header, Sidebar, and BottomNav components. Explain the component hierarchy, prop drilling patterns, and component composition strategies. Detail the routing system with protected routes, nested routes, and page transitions using Motion animations. Document the responsive design implementation, mobile-first approach, and cross-device compatibility. Include component lifecycle management, error boundaries, and performance optimization techniques. Provide guidelines for component reusability, prop interfaces, and TypeScript integration patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Extension Architecture & Manifest
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Chrome extension architecture and manifest configuration. Detail the Manifest V3 structure including permissions, host permissions, background service worker setup, and content script injection. Explain the extension's security model, permission requirements for tabs, storage, clipboard access, and system display monitoring. Document the service worker lifecycle, background script functionality for persistent monitoring, and integration with browser APIs. Include manifest configuration best practices, security considerations for production deployments, and browser compatibility requirements. Address the extension's role in the overall ExamGuard Pro ecosystem and its relationship with the main backend services.

wiki_catalogs.name: Computer Vision Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive computer vision services documentation for ExamGuard Pro focused on real-time visual analysis capabilities. Document the MediaPipe-based face detection system including dual-backend architecture (Tasks API and Haar cascades), face landmark extraction, and presence verification algorithms. Explain the YOLOv8-powered object detection system for identifying unauthorized devices, prohibited items, and suspicious objects. Cover gaze tracking implementation using eye landmark analysis and attention monitoring. Detail frame extraction techniques for periodic analysis and video stream processing. Include configuration parameters for detection thresholds, model loading strategies, fallback mechanisms, and performance optimization. Document integration patterns with the main event processing pipeline and real-time WebSocket communication.

wiki_catalogs.name: WebSocket Communication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for WebSocket communication patterns in ExamGuard Pro. Document the RealtimeMonitoringManager architecture including connection pooling for dashboards, proctors, and students. Explain the room-based session management system and how WebSocket connections are established for /ws/dashboard, /ws/proctor, and /ws/student endpoints. Detail the event broadcasting mechanisms including RealtimeEvent data structures, message serialization, and connection lifecycle management. Include concrete examples from the codebase showing WebSocket handshake procedures, connection acceptance, and graceful disconnection handling. Document the heartbeat mechanism for connection health monitoring and the binary data streaming for video feeds. Address WebSocket security considerations, connection limits, and error handling strategies. Provide practical examples of client-side WebSocket integration patterns and real-time data consumption.

wiki_catalogs.name: Entity Relationship Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive entity relationship documentation for ExamGuard Pro's core data models. Detail the ExamSession entity with fields for session_id, student_id, start_time, end_time, status, and risk_score. Document the Student entity including student_id, user_id, registration_date, and profile information. Explain the Event entity covering event_id, session_id, event_type, timestamp, and metadata. Detail the AnalysisResult entity with analysis_id, session_id, ai_model, confidence_score, and findings. Document the Research entity containing research_id, session_id, data_points, and analysis_results. Show foreign key relationships, primary keys, and indexing strategies. Include entity relationship diagrams illustrating how sessions connect to students, events, and analysis results. Document business rules, validation constraints, and referential integrity requirements for each entity.

wiki_catalogs.name: Face Detection System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the face detection system in ExamGuard Pro. Detail the dual-backend architecture supporting both MediaPipe Tasks API and Haar cascade classifiers, including automatic fallback mechanisms. Document the SecureVision class implementation, face landmark extraction processes, and presence verification algorithms. Explain detection threshold configurations, model loading strategies, and performance optimization techniques. Cover violation detection for multiple faces, face absence monitoring, and side-looking detection. Include practical examples of frame analysis, bounding box calculations, and integrity score impact assessments. Document integration with the main event processing pipeline and real-time WebSocket communication patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: OCR Text Extraction & Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive OCR text extraction and processing documentation for ExamGuard Pro focused on screen capture analysis. Document the Tesseract OCR implementation including installation requirements, configuration for Windows systems, and fallback mechanisms when Tesseract is unavailable. Explain the text extraction process from screenshots, including image preprocessing, text normalization, and keyword detection algorithms. Detail the forbidden keyword detection system with configurable keyword lists and risk scoring calculations. Cover the async execution model using thread pools for non-blocking OCR processing. Include configuration options for Tesseract path settings, OCR quality parameters, and performance optimization techniques. Document error handling strategies, text truncation limits, and integration with the overall AI analysis pipeline.

wiki_catalogs.name: Event Capture Flow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Chrome extension event capture flow in ExamGuard Pro. Document how user actions are captured through content scripts, background script coordination, and event logging mechanisms. Explain the event types captured including copy/paste operations, tab switching, navigation events, window focus changes, and camera/webcam detections. Detail the data transformation process from raw browser events to structured event objects with timestamps, session IDs, and contextual data. Document the event queuing mechanism, batch processing strategies, and real-time transmission to the backend via WebSocket connections. Include security considerations for event capture, privacy protection measures, and data anonymization techniques. Provide examples of event payloads and explain how different event types trigger specific analysis workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Background Script Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Chrome extension background script architecture. Thoroughly explain the background script's role as the central coordinator for all extension activities, including tab monitoring, event logging, and WebSocket communication management. Document the event capture mechanisms for detecting tab changes, navigation events, and browser activity. Detail the eventLogger.js integration for systematic event recording and the manifest.json configuration that defines extension permissions and background script lifecycle. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing how the background script manages multiple tabs simultaneously, handles extension updates, and maintains persistent state. Explain the relationship between the background script and other extension components, including data flow patterns and error handling strategies. Address security considerations, permission management, and debugging techniques for background script development.

wiki_catalogs.name: Connection Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for WebSocket connection management in ExamGuard Pro. Document the RealtimeMonitoringManager architecture including three connection pools: dashboard_connections, proctor_connections, and student_connections. Explain the connection establishment process for /ws/dashboard, /ws/proctor, and /ws/student endpoints with proper WebSocket.accept() handling. Detail the RoomManager class for session-based room management and how connections are added to rooms using join_room() and leave_room() methods. Include concrete examples from the codebase showing connection acceptance, role-based connection handling, and graceful disconnection cleanup. Document the connection statistics tracking, total connection counting, and connection lifecycle management. Address connection limits, error handling during connection establishment, and the circular import handling for AI engine access. Provide practical examples of client-side connection establishment patterns and connection state management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Pipeline Coordination & Worker Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the AnalysisPipeline coordination and worker management system. Thoroughly explain the singleton pattern implementation, background worker lifecycle management, and queue-based event processing architecture. Document the start/stop mechanisms, task cancellation handling, and graceful shutdown procedures. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing worker initialization, queue management, and error handling strategies. Explain the statistics tracking system for monitoring pipeline performance including events_processed, transformer_analyses, db_updates, and errors counters. Detail the timeout handling for queue operations and the retry mechanisms for failed processing attempts. Address memory management, resource cleanup, and the relationship between pipeline workers and concurrent event processing. Provide examples of extending the worker pool and handling high-volume event scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: WebSocket Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the WebSocket architecture in ExamGuard Pro. Explain the RealtimeMonitoringManager class and its role as the central coordinator for all WebSocket connections. Document the three connection pools: dashboard connections for administrators, proctor connections for invigilators, and student connections for examinees. Detail the RoomManager implementation for session-based room hierarchies and how it enables targeted broadcasting. Include concrete examples of connection establishment, message routing patterns, and the multi-tiered broadcasting system. Explain the connection lifecycle management, error handling strategies, and automatic cleanup mechanisms. Document the WebSocket message formats, event types, and the structured approach to real-time communication between all system components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for ExamGuard Pro focused on rapid deployment and initial setup. Document prerequisites including Python 3.10+, Node.js 18+, Supabase account requirements, and Tesseract OCR installation. wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed technology stack documentation for ExamGuard Pro covering all major technologies and their roles in the system. Document backend technologies including FastAPI, Python 3.10+, Supabase (PostgreSQL), and asyncpg. Cover frontend stack with React 19, Vite, Tailwind CSS, and Lucide Icons. Detail AI/ML technologies including MediaPipe for face detection, YOLOv8 for object detection, Tesseract OCR for text recognition, and Sentence-transformers for NLP. Document Chrome extension technologies including Manifest V3, WebRTC, and background scripts. Include deployment technologies like Docker, Render, and production considerations. Provide version compatibility information and rationale for technology choices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Interactions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for component interaction patterns in ExamGuard Pro system. Thoroughly explain how the Chrome extension captures user events and browser activities, how these events are transmitted through WebSocket connections to the FastAPI backend, and how the backend processes them through the AI analysis pipeline. Document the real-time communication flow between dashboard, proctor, and student WebSocket endpoints. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing event routing, message formatting, and connection management. Explain relationships with other components including the role of the RealtimeMonitoringManager in coordinating connections and the Pipeline service in orchestrating AI analysis workflows. Address common integration issues like connection timeouts, message queuing, and event ordering. Make content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for experienced developers working on system integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Session Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for ExamGuard Pro exam session management endpoints focused on session lifecycle control and monitoring. Include error handling for session conflicts, capacity limits, and scheduling conflicts. Address session monitoring patterns, real-time updates via WebSocket, and integration with the Chrome extension for live monitoring.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed state management documentation for the ExamGuard Pro React application. Document the Context API implementation including AppContext for global application state and AuthContext for authentication state. Document custom hooks like useWebSocket for reactive state updates and data fetching patterns. Include state persistence strategies, memory management, and performance considerations for real-time updates. Provide examples of state consumption patterns and best practices for avoiding unnecessary re-renders.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Script Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for content script integration and DOM manipulation capabilities. wiki_catalogs.name: NLP & Text Analysis Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed NLP and text analysis services documentation for ExamGuard Pro focused on content monitoring and plagiarism detection capabilities. Explain the transformer-based text similarity analysis using sentence-transformers for detecting copied content and paraphrased violations. Include configuration options for OCR engines, similarity thresholds, model selection criteria, and performance tuning parameters. Document the integration with the AI pipeline and risk scoring algorithms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Event Routing & Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for event routing and processing workflows in ExamGuard Pro. Thoroughly explain how the Pipeline service coordinates AI analysis workflows and routes processed events to appropriate WebSocket subscribers. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing event transformation, risk scoring calculations, and multi-tier alert systems. Explain the relationship between RealtimeEvent structures and the Pipeline's processing stages. Detail the event history management, late-joiner notification delivery, and event deduplication strategies. Address performance considerations for high-throughput event processing and the integration between synchronous AI analysis callbacks and asynchronous WebSocket broadcasting. Provide examples of extending the pipeline with custom analysis modules and handling processing failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Schemas and Validation

Document validation error handling and user-friendly error messaging strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Script Injection
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for content script injection mechanisms in the Chrome extension. Provide troubleshooting guidance for injection failures and performance optimization techniques for screen capture operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Event Broadcasting
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for real-time event broadcasting mechanisms in ExamGuard Pro. Document the RealtimeEvent data structure with event_type, student_id, session_id, data, alert_level, and timestamp fields. Explain the EventType enumeration covering session events, monitoring events, suspicious activities, advanced detection layers, analysis events, system events, and heartbeat messages. Detail the broadcast_event() method for sending events to relevant subscribers including dashboard connections, session-specific proctors, and room members. Document the convenience methods for common events like notify_face_missing(), notify_suspicious_activity(), notify_plagiarism(), and notify_risk_update(). Include examples of event serialization using RealtimeEvent.to_json(), message preparation, and broadcasting to multiple connection pools. Address event history management, late-joiner notification delivery, and the _broadcast_to_set() helper method for efficient message distribution. Provide practical examples of event-driven communication patterns and subscriber filtering logic.

wiki_catalogs.name: Event Processing Stages
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the event processing stages within the AnalysisPipeline. Thoroughly explain the event routing mechanism that directs different event types to appropriate handlers including text events (COPY, PASTE, CLIPBOARD_TEXT), navigation events (TAB_SWITCH, NAVIGATION), focus events (WINDOW_BLUR, PAGE_HIDDEN), transformer alerts, and vision events (FACE_ABSENT, PHONE_DETECTED). Document the event transformation process, input validation, and the sequential processing flow from event reception to analysis completion. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing event type detection, parameter extraction, and handler delegation. Explain the event data structure requirements, timestamp handling, and session context preservation throughout the processing pipeline. Detail the batch processing capabilities for related events and the coordination between synchronous AI analysis callbacks and asynchronous processing stages. Address event ordering guarantees, processing timeouts, and error propagation mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frame Extraction Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the frame extraction pipeline that processes live video streams for AI analysis. Document the buffer management system, frame rate control algorithms, and the integration with AI analysis callbacks. Include concrete examples from the codebase showing how video streams are segmented, buffered, and processed asynchronously. Explain the thread-safe communication patterns between the frame extractor and the AI analysis engines. Detail the performance optimization techniques for handling high-throughput video streams and the resource management strategies for memory-efficient frame processing. Address the callback integration system that triggers AI analysis upon frame availability.

Detail the dual-path video delivery: forwarding chunks to AI analysis engines and relaying raw video chunks directly to clients. Document the session-based routing where video streams are broadcast to room members and dashboard connections. Include examples of binary data handling, video chunk processing, and the cleanup of stream buffers when connections terminate. Address the frame extraction pipeline, AI engine callbacks, and the coordination between video streaming and real-time analysis. Provide practical examples of client-side video reception, binary data handling, and integration with the Chrome extension's video capture functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: Risk Scoring & Session Updates
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the risk scoring integration and session management within the AnalysisPipeline. Thoroughly explain the automatic risk score calculation, risk level determination (safe, review, suspicious), and session state updates triggered by various events. Document the risk score thresholds, penalty calculations, and the relationship between different violation types and their impact on session metrics including engagement_score, effort_alignment, and content_relevance. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing risk score modifications for forbidden site visits, plagiarism detection, and vision anomalies. Explain the session data structure updates, database transaction handling, and the real-time broadcasting of risk level changes to connected clients. Detail the integration with Supabase for persistent storage, the session context preservation across updates, and the cascading effects of risk score changes on session status. Address the balance between immediate risk updates and batch processing optimizations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Event History System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the event history system that maintains real-time event records for late-joiner synchronization. Explain the event history buffer management, including the configurable maximum history size and circular buffer implementation. Document how new connections receive recent event history to synchronize their state with ongoing sessions. Detail the RealtimeEvent data structure, event serialization mechanisms, and the JSON encoding/decoding processes. Include concrete examples of event history retrieval, the _send_history method implementation, and how the system handles event replay for new subscribers. Explain the event filtering and deduplication strategies, the timestamp-based ordering system, and the memory management approaches for efficient history storage.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend API Documentation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for ExamGuard Pro RESTful endpoints focused on the FastAPI backend services. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication methods for all public endpoints including authentication, sessions, students, reports, and analysis services. Include detailed endpoint specifications for user management, exam session control, real-time event processing, and AI analysis results. Document WebSocket endpoints for real-time communication including connection handling, message formats, event types, and client integration patterns. Provide comprehensive examples of request/response payloads, error handling strategies, security considerations, and rate limiting. Include authentication flows using JWT tokens, authorization patterns, and API versioning information. Address common use cases, client implementation guidelines, and performance optimization tips for integrating with external systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed core features documentation for ExamGuard Pro highlighting the primary capabilities that ensure academic integrity. Document real-time monitoring features including tab switching detection, window blurring events, and copy/paste tracking. Explain AI-driven analysis capabilities covering face detection with MediaPipe, gaze tracking for attention monitoring, OCR analysis for forbidden content detection, object detection for unauthorized device monitoring, and text similarity for plagiarism detection. Detail the dynamic risk scoring system with weighted event scoring and live risk levels (Safe, Review, Suspicious). Document interactive dashboard features with real-time WebSocket updates, student performance metrics, and session timelines. Include robust reporting capabilities with automated PDF generation and violation logging. Provide practical examples of each feature in action and their integration within the overall system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Design Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for design patterns used in ExamGuard Pro system. Describe the microservices architecture with clear separation of concerns between AI analysis services, real-time communication, and data persistence. Document the singleton pattern implementation for AI service instances and pipeline management, observer pattern usage in WebSocket real-time updates, and factory pattern for AI service creation. Explain the pipeline pattern for event processing workflows and how these patterns contribute to system scalability and maintainability. Include infrastructure requirements, scalability considerations, and deployment topology. Provide system context diagrams showing component relationships and integration patterns. Address cross-cutting concerns like error handling, logging, and resource management. Document technology stack decisions, third-party dependencies, and version compatibility requirements. Explain how design patterns enable extensibility for adding new AI analysis modules and WebSocket endpoints.

wiki_catalogs.name: Analysis Services API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for ExamGuard Pro AI analysis service endpoints focused on real-time monitoring data processing and risk assessment. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, and request/response schemas for /api/analysis endpoints including event analysis, risk scoring, and violation detection. Include detailed specifications for analysis parameters, confidence thresholds, and automated decision-making processes. Document the integration with computer vision services, OCR processing, and NLP analysis modules. Provide comprehensive examples of analysis request formats, risk score calculations, and violation categorization. Include error handling for analysis failures, model unavailability, and processing timeouts. Address analysis result interpretation, manual override capabilities, and integration with dashboard reporting for real-time monitoring and alert generation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Routing & Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive routing and navigation documentation for the ExamGuard Pro React application. Document the React Router configuration including protected routes, nested routing, and route guards. Explain navigation patterns, breadcrumb implementation, and active link highlighting. Detail the sidebar navigation structure, bottom navigation for mobile devices, and menu item organization. Document programmatic navigation, route parameters handling, and query string management. Include navigation accessibility features, keyboard shortcuts, and focus management. Provide examples of conditional rendering based on user roles and navigation state management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Popup Interface & User Controls
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the extension's popup interface and user interaction controls. Detail the popup HTML structure, CSS styling, and JavaScript functionality for session management and user controls. Explain the popup's role in exam session initiation, status monitoring, and user notifications. Document the interface elements for starting/stopping monitoring, viewing session status, accessing settings, and handling user feedback. Include responsive design considerations, accessibility features, and cross-browser compatibility. Address the popup's integration with background script functionality, real-time status updates, and user preference management. Provide guidance on popup customization, branding options, and user experience optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Transformer Model Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed transformer model implementation documentation for ExamGuard Pro focused on the custom neural network architecture used for text analysis and sequence modeling. Document the transformer model architecture including multi-head attention mechanisms, positional encoding, and feed-forward layers. Explain the encoder-decoder structure designed for exam content analysis and behavioral pattern recognition. Cover the embedding systems for tokenization, vocabulary management, and sequence representation. Detail the training configuration, hyperparameters, and optimization strategies. Include model checkpoint management, inference procedures, and performance benchmarking. Document the integration with the main AI pipeline and data preprocessing workflows. Address model deployment considerations and inference optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-Time Coordination
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for real-time coordination mechanisms in ExamGuard Pro. Thoroughly explain how the RealtimeMonitoringManager coordinates multiple concurrent WebSocket connections and manages session-based room hierarchies. Document the frame extraction pipeline for processing live video streams including buffer management, frame rate control, and AI analysis callback integration. Include concrete examples from the codebase showing the coordination between video streaming, AI analysis execution, and real-time event broadcasting. Explain the heartbeat monitoring system for connection health, the event history management for late-joiner synchronization, and the statistical tracking of connection metrics. Detail the integration with AI services for concurrent processing of multiple video streams and the thread-safe communication patterns used for cross-service coordination. Address performance optimization techniques for handling high-concurrency scenarios and the resource management strategies for video stream processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Lifecycle Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for ExamGuard Pro's data lifecycle management including retention policies, archival strategies, and cleanup procedures. Document data retention periods for different entity types including exam sessions, student records, event logs, and analysis results. Explain automated cleanup processes for expired sessions and temporary data. Detail backup and restore procedures for critical exam data. Document data archiving strategies for historical analysis and compliance requirements. Include data anonymization procedures for privacy protection and GDPR compliance. Explain cache management strategies for frequently accessed data including session information and student profiles. Document data migration procedures for schema updates and system upgrades. Address disaster recovery planning and business continuity measures for critical exam data. Include monitoring and alerting for data integrity and availability.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frame Extraction Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the frame extraction and video processing engine. Document the frame sampling strategies, timing intervals, and quality optimization techniques used for periodic analysis. Explain integration with webcam capture systems, screen recording workflows, and real-time video stream processing. Cover frame preprocessing steps including resizing, normalization, and format conversion. Detail the relationship with other computer vision services and event triggering mechanisms. Include practical examples of frame acquisition timing, memory management for continuous streams, and performance optimization for high-frequency analysis. Document configuration options for frame rates, resolution settings, and storage optimization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Research & Content Authenticity Analysis
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for research and content authenticity analysis services in ExamGuard Pro. Document the academic integrity checking mechanisms, including source verification, citation analysis, and content authenticity validation. Explain the research analysis algorithms used to detect unauthorized collaboration, content sharing, and academic dishonesty patterns. Detail the integration with external databases and academic resources for content verification. Cover the plagiarism detection workflows, including multi-source comparison, similarity clustering, and pattern recognition algorithms. Include configuration options for research database connections, verification thresholds, and false positive reduction techniques. Document the reporting mechanisms for research violations and integration with institutional academic policies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for database integration patterns in ExamGuard Pro. Document the Supabase PostgreSQL integration, real-time database features, and data synchronization mechanisms. Explain the data models for exam sessions, events, analysis results, and student profiles including relationships and constraints. Detail the real-time subscription patterns for live data updates, change data capture mechanisms, and conflict resolution strategies. Document the data access patterns, query optimization techniques, and caching strategies for high-performance real-time applications. Include data lifecycle management, retention policies, and archival procedures. Explain the transaction handling, consistency guarantees, and backup/recovery mechanisms. Address data security measures, encryption at rest and in transit, and access control implementations. Provide examples of common queries, data insertion patterns, and real-time data broadcasting.

wiki_catalogs.name: WebSocket Broadcasting & Client Notifications
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the WebSocket broadcasting and client notification system within the AnalysisPipeline. Thoroughly explain the real-time event distribution mechanism, alert level categorization (INFO, WARNING, CRITICAL), and the integration with the RealtimeEvent system for multi-client communication. Document the broadcast_event method, room-based messaging for exam sessions, and the client-side event handling patterns. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing alert level determination, event type mapping, and the data payload structure sent to dashboards and extensions. Explain the relationship between session_id and exam_id in broadcast routing, the student_session_id preservation for client context, and the alert level escalation based on event severity. Detail the WebSocket connection management, message queuing for disconnected clients, and the real-time dashboard updates including risk score changes and violation notifications. Address the integration with the Chrome extension for immediate anomaly responses and the coordination between multiple client types.

wiki_catalogs.name: Heartbeat Monitoring
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the heartbeat monitoring system that ensures connection health and system status tracking. Explain the periodic heartbeat mechanism, including configurable intervals and the automated heartbeat message generation. Document the heartbeat message structure, containing connection statistics and system metrics. Detail the connection health monitoring, automatic disconnection detection, and cleanup procedures. Include concrete examples of heartbeat implementation, the _send_heartbeat method, and the stats collection system. Explain how heartbeat messages are used for system monitoring, connection quality assessment, and performance tracking. Address the heartbeat-based connection management strategies and the integration with the broader real-time coordination system.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI/ML Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive AI/ML services documentation for ExamGuard Pro focused on the multi-modal analysis capabilities including face detection, object detection, OCR text analysis, and NLP-based plagiarism detection. Document the implementation details of MediaPipe-based face detection and presence verification, YOLOv8-powered object detection for unauthorized devices, Tesseract OCR for content analysis, and transformer models for text similarity and behavioral analysis. Explain the AI pipeline architecture showing how events trigger analysis workflows, risk scoring algorithms, and decision-making processes. Include configuration options for AI models, performance tuning parameters, and accuracy thresholds. Document the integration patterns between AI services and the main event processing pipeline. Address model training procedures, inference optimization, and deployment considerations for production environments.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for ExamGuard Pro reporting and analytics endpoints focused on PDF report generation, session analytics, and performance metrics. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, and request/response schemas for /api/reports endpoints including report generation, analytics data export, and statistical summaries. Include detailed specifications for report templates, data filtering options, and export formats. Document analytics endpoints for session performance, student behavior patterns, and system usage metrics. Provide comprehensive examples of report generation requests, analytics query parameters, and data export responses. Include error handling for report generation failures, large data exports, and template rendering issues. Address report customization options, scheduled report generation, and integration with external analytics systems for comprehensive exam monitoring insights.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed real-time integration documentation for the ExamGuard Pro React dashboard. Explain the event-driven processing model, service coordination, and result aggregation mechanisms. Detail the connection pooling statistics, event broadcasting metrics, and historical data tracking. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed frontend dashboard documentation for ExamGuard Pro React application focused on the interactive monitoring interface and real-time data visualization. Include component props, events, and customization options for each UI element. Provide guidelines for responsive design, accessibility compliance, and cross-browser compatibility. Document the real-time data update mechanisms, WebSocket message handling, and dashboard analytics visualization. Include detailed specifications for event broadcasting, room management, and client authentication. Document WebSocket message protocols for real-time monitoring updates, session status changes, and alert notifications. Provide comprehensive examples of WebSocket connection establishment, message handling patterns, and reconnection strategies. Address WebSocket security considerations, rate limiting, and scalability patterns for handling multiple concurrent connections in real-time exam monitoring environments.

Document the extension architecture including background script functionality, content script integration, and manifest V3 configuration. Explain the core monitoring capabilities including tab switching detection, window blurring events, copy/paste tracking, screen capture functionality, and webcam video processing. Document the event logging system, data transmission to backend services, and WebRTC signaling support. Address data security, privacy requirements, and access control mechanisms for protecting sensitive exam information.

Address security best practices including SSL/TLS configuration, secret management, and network security. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines documentation for ExamGuard Pro focused on code standards, testing strategies, and contribution workflows. Explain the testing approach including unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end testing strategies. Include debugging techniques for multi-modal AI services, WebSocket communication issues, and real-time data synchronization problems. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common development issues including dependency conflicts, environment setup problems, and cross-platform compatibility. Include contribution guidelines for open-source collaboration and release management procedures.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# ExamGuard Pro - AI-Powered Proctoring System

## Project Introduction

ExamGuard Pro is an advanced, real-time exam monitoring and proctoring system that leverages artificial intelligence to ensure academic integrity during online examinations. The system provides comprehensive surveillance through multi-modal analysis including face detection, eye gaze tracking, OCR-based content monitoring, and NLP-based plagiarism detection.

### Core Goals and Objectives

- **Academic Integrity**: Monitor and prevent cheating during online exams through real-time surveillance
- **Multi-Modal Analysis**: Integrate various AI technologies for comprehensive exam monitoring
- **Real-Time Processing**: Provide immediate feedback and alerts during exam sessions
- **Comprehensive Reporting**: Generate detailed violation logs and automated PDF reports
- **Scalable Architecture**: Support multiple concurrent exam sessions with WebSocket-based communication

### Target Audience

- Educational institutions implementing online proctoring solutions
- Academic administrators managing exam integrity
- Proctors monitoring multiple students simultaneously
- Students taking online examinations under supervision

## Technical Architecture

The system follows a microservices architecture with clear separation between frontend, backend, AI processing, and browser extension components.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Chrome Extension] --> B[WebSocket API]
    C[React Dashboard] --> B
    B --> D[FastAPI Backend]
    D --> E[AI/ML Services]
    D --> F[Supabase Database]
    E --> G[Face Detection]
    E --> H[OCR Analysis]
    E --> I[NLP Processing]
    E --> J[Object Detection]
    K[Transformer Models] --> I
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Layer**
- React-based dashboard with real-time monitoring capabilities
- Responsive design with Tailwind CSS styling
- WebSocket integration for live updates

**Backend Layer**
- FastAPI application with async/await support
- WebSocket endpoints for real-time communication
- RESTful API for administrative functions

**AI/ML Layer**
- Multi-modal analysis pipeline
- Computer vision for face detection and object recognition
- Natural language processing for content analysis
- OCR for text extraction and forbidden content detection

**Browser Extension**
- Chrome extension for real-time monitoring
- Screen capture and webcam functionality
- Event logging and data transmission

### Design Patterns

The system implements several key design patterns:

- **Singleton Pattern**: Used for AI service instances and pipeline management
- **Observer Pattern**: Implemented in WebSocket connections for real-time updates
- **Factory Pattern**: Used for creating different AI analysis services
- **Pipeline Pattern**: Structured event processing through analysis stages

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Backend Entry Point**
- `server/main.py` - Primary FastAPI application with WebSocket endpoints and real-time monitoring

**Frontend Entry Point**
- `examguard-pro/src/App.tsx` - React application with routing and authentication context

**Extension Entry Point**
- `extension/background.js` - Chrome extension background script with session management

### Core Modules

**Real-time Analysis Pipeline**
- `server/services/pipeline.py` - Centralized event processing and risk scoring
- Implements async queue-based processing for handling multiple concurrent events

**AI Service Integration**
- `server/services/face_detection.py` - MediaPipe-based face detection and analysis
- `server/services/ocr.py` - Tesseract OCR for screen content analysis
- `server/services/object_detection.py` - YOLO-based object recognition

**WebSocket Communication**
- `server/services/realtime.py` - Real-time messaging and session management
- Handles bidirectional communication between students, proctors, and dashboard

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Configuration**
- `server/config.py` - Centralized configuration with environment variable support
- Database connection management and AI model settings
- Risk scoring thresholds and analysis parameters

**Build Configuration**
- `Dockerfile` - Multi-stage Docker build for frontend and backend
- `examguard-pro/package.json` - React application dependencies and scripts
- `transformer/config.py` - AI model configuration and hyperparameters

### External Dependencies

**Backend Dependencies**
- FastAPI 0.104+ for web framework
- Async database operations with asyncpg
- MediaPipe for computer vision tasks
- Supabase for real-time database operations

**Frontend Dependencies**
- React 19 with modern hooks and context API
- Vite for build tooling and hot module replacement
- Tailwind CSS for responsive styling
- Motion for smooth animations and transitions

**AI/ML Dependencies**
- PyTesseract for OCR text recognition
- OpenCV for computer vision operations
- Torch for transformer model inference
- Sentence-transformers for semantic similarity

### Integration Points

**WebSocket Architecture**
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Student as Student Browser
    participant Extension as Chrome Extension
    participant Backend as FastAPI Server
    participant Dashboard as React Dashboard
    
    Student->>Extension: Event Data
    Extension->>Backend: WebSocket Message
    Backend->>Backend: Process Event
    Backend->>Dashboard: Real-time Update
    Backend->>Student: Response/Alert
```

**Data Flow Architecture**
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Events] --> B[Chrome Extension]
    B --> C[WebSocket Connection]
    C --> D[FastAPI Backend]
    D --> E[Analysis Pipeline]
    E --> F[AI Services]
    F --> G[Database Updates]
    G --> H[Real-time Broadcasting]
    H --> I[Dashboard Updates]
    H --> J[Student Notifications]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Multi-Modal Monitoring**
- Face detection and presence verification using MediaPipe
- Eye gaze tracking for attention monitoring
- Screen capture and OCR analysis for forbidden content detection
- Object detection for unauthorized devices using YOLOv8
- Plagiarism detection through NLP-based text similarity

**Real-Time Processing**
- Dynamic risk scoring with weighted event analysis
- Live WebSocket updates for dashboard monitoring
- Interactive session timelines and analytics
- Automated PDF report generation

**Advanced Analytics**
- Behavioral pattern analysis and anomaly detection
- Browsing behavior tracking with risk categorization
- Engagement and effort score calculations
- Comprehensive violation logging and reporting

### Implementation Highlights

**Risk Scoring System**
- `server/config.py` - Configurable risk weightings and thresholds
- Real-time calculation of safety levels (Safe, Review, Suspicious)
- Dynamic adjustment based on event patterns and severity

**WebSocket Communication**
- `server/main.py` - Multiple WebSocket endpoints for different roles
- Session-based room management for targeted communications
- Binary data streaming for video/audio capture

**AI Pipeline Integration**
- `server/services/pipeline.py` - Centralized event processing workflow
- Modular service architecture for independent AI components
- Batch processing and real-time analysis coordination

### Feature Architecture

**State Management Flow**
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> SessionStarted
    SessionStarted --> MonitoringActive: Student Connected
    MonitoringActive --> RiskAssessment: Event Detected
    RiskAssessment --> Safe: Low Risk
    RiskAssessment --> Review: Medium Risk
    RiskAssessment --> Suspicious: High Risk
    Safe --> MonitoringActive: Continue
    Review --> MonitoringActive: Monitor
    Suspicious --> AlertTriggered: Trigger Proctor Action
    AlertTriggered --> MonitoringActive: Resolution
    MonitoringActive --> SessionEnded: Exam Complete
    SessionEnded --> [*]
```

**Component Relationship Diagram**
```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Chrome Extension] --> B[Event Capture]
    B --> C[WebSocket Transport]
    C --> D[FastAPI Router]
    D --> E[Analysis Pipeline]
    E --> F[AI Services]
    F --> G[Database Layer]
    G --> H[Real-time Broadcasting]
    H --> I[React Dashboard]
    H --> J[Student Interface]
```

The ExamGuard Pro system represents a comprehensive solution for modern online exam proctoring, combining cutting-edge AI technologies with robust real-time communication infrastructure to ensure academic integrity in digital learning environments.

**Sources:**
- [README.md](README.md)
- [server/main.py](server/main.py)
- [examguard-pro/src/App.tsx](examguard-pro/src/App.tsx)
- [extension/background.js](extension/background.js)
- [server/config.py](server/config.py)
- [server/services/pipeline.py](server/services/pipeline.py)
- [server/services/face_detection.py](server/services/face_detection.py)
- [server/services/ocr.py](server/services/ocr.py)
- [transformer/config.py](transformer/config.py)
- [Dockerfile](Dockerfile)
- [server/requirements.txt](server/requirements.txt)
- [examguard-pro/package.json](examguard-pro/package.json)
- [transformer/requirements.txt](transformer/requirements.txt)

</blog>
